title: Listen to the Latest ‘KFF Health News Minute’
summary: The "KFF Health News Minute" brings original health care and health policy reporting from our newsroom to the airwaves each week.

title: Louisiana’s Reporting Law Chills Immigrant Medicaid Applications
summary: A year after the measure’s passage, a state law is keeping immigrants and their children from accessing Medicaid even when they qualify.

title: RFK Jr. Seeks To Peek at Americans’ Medical Records for Clues on Autism and Vaccines
summary: To collect and scrutinize millions of Americans’ health data, U.S. health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. aims to work with state organizations that help health systems share medical records. In Nebraska, millions in federal dollars has flowed into one nonprofit cooperating with Kennedy’s project.

title: Trump’s Medicaid Work Rules Force States To Scrap Plans and Rework Systems
summary: Health experts and advocates for low-income people say federal rules implementing President Donald Trump’s new Medicaid work requirements upend months of work by state governments to prepare the computer systems that determine who’s eligible for benefits.

title: Michigan Found a Way To Reduce School Vaccine Waivers. Until It Backfired.
summary: The state had high rates of parents not vaccinating their children, so it started making them attend vaccine education sessions to opt out their kids. It seemed to work. Then things got ugly.

title: At a Tennessee Hospital, a Nurse Stole Fentanyl and AI Missed It, State Records Say
summary: Sentri7, drug diversion software powered by artificial intelligence and used at hundreds of U.S. hospitals, did not catch a months-long string of fentanyl thefts in Tennessee in 2025, according to a state document.

title: Festering Infections to Untreated Cancer: ICE Detainees Describe Medical Neglect Across US
summary: Immigrant detainees have told courts across the nation that detention officials have failed to treat or stabilize their conditions, from pregnancy to prostate cancer, suggesting that systemic lapses in care extend well beyond record deaths in Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody.

title: Focused on Work, Needed at Home: A Federal Caregiving Policy Might Help
summary: The Family and Medical Leave Act gives eligible employees up to 12 weeks of job-protected leave for caregiving. But the federal policy has noteworthy limitations. The HealthQ team explains.

title: Telehealth Booms as Demand for GLP-1s Surges and Questions Mount About Safety, Oversight
summary: Many telehealth companies have emerged in recent years offering easy access to GLP-1 weight loss drugs as demand has exploded. Meanwhile, researchers and doctors are concerned that some of these online companies aren't properly screening or monitoring patients. “It gives a black eye to telemedicine,” one researcher said.

title: Amid Ebola, Hantavirus Outbreaks, Democrats Decry Trump’s Health Cuts
summary: Infectious disease specialists say the viruses are unlikely to become pandemics, but some are still raising concerns about the federal health response and what it portends should a pandemic similar to or worse than covid occur.
